text:
The US SPR declined 3mm bbls last week.

The main draw was in the Sour side (Medium or Heavy).   

Light oil (Sweet) is well supplied, so not wanted from the  SPR.

The question is how much more Sour oil can be drawn below 200 mmbls (where it is now). #OOTT

The bank says data-centre copper demand may arrive more slowly because of grid constraints, equipment shortages, public opposition, project delays and greater use of optical connectivity.

Fair point.

But slower does not mean zero.
